name: Argüellos Biosphere: Curueño and Torío Basins Loop
name_original: Reserva de la Biosfera de los Argüellos – circular por las cuencas del Curueño y el Torío
description: This difficult 29.9-mile touring cycling route through the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Argüellos offers dramatic gorges and mountain views
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vegacervera traverse a landscape defined by dramatic natural features. The region is characterized by the imposing limestone cliffs of the Hoces de Vegacervera gorge, carved by the Torío River, and features varied terrain with significant elevation changes. Cyclists encounter winding roads, river valleys, and areas of lush forests. This environment offers a range of routes for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Vegacervera The most popular touring cycling route is Argüellos Biosphere: Curueño and Torío Basins Loop, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This challenging route showcases dramatic gorges and mountain views within the biosphere reserve. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop, a difficult 32.5 miles (52.3 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and extensive views across the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. Local touring cyclists also love the La Pola de Gordón Town Center – Hoces de Vegacervera loop from Vegacervera, a 47.7 miles (76.8 km) trail leading through the Hoces de Vegacervera, often completed in about 5 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Vegacervera is defined by deep gorges, towering limestone cliffs, and the winding Torío River. The network offers options for both easy rides and more challenging routes with significant ascents. The routes in Vegacervera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 9 reviews. More than 270 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vegacervera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of touring cycling routes can I expect around Vegacervera?
A: Vegacervera offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, predominantly characterized by dramatic natural features. You'll encounter the imposing limestone cliffs of the Hoces de Vegacervera gorge, carved by the Torío River, winding roads, and areas of lush forests. The terrain often includes significant elevation changes, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Vegacervera for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in Vegacervera are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For a less strenuous ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10504177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Robla Town Center – Ermita del Buen Suceso loop from La Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 18.8 km and takes approximately 1 hour 17 minutes. This route offers a gentler introduction to the region's beauty.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vegacervera are rich with natural landmarks. You'll frequently encounter the breathtaking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5505126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces de Vegacervera&lt;/a&gt;, with its towering limestone cliffs and the Torío River. Other notable natural features in the vicinity include the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt; and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6040633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nocedo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, Vegacervera features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037090159/argueellos-biosphere-curueno-and-torio-basins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argüellos Biosphere: Curueño and Torío Basins Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 48.1 km long and showcases dramatic gorges and mountain views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037344610/alto-bernesga-biosphere-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.3 km route offering extensive views.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Vegacervera?
A: The touring cycling routes around Vegacervera are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic gorges, stunning mountain views, and the immersive experience of cycling through nature, far from heavy traffic.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in the Vegacervera area?
A: There are 15 touring cycling routes available around Vegacervera, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vegacervera?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the region's lush forests and riverbanks suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer would also be suitable, though it might be warmer, especially in the gorges. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The majority of touring cycling routes in Vegacervera are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and demanding terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – Hoces de Vegacervera loop from Vegacervera&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 76.8 km ride with considerable ascents, offering a rewarding challenge.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through mountain passes or offer high-altitude views?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Vegacervera includes routes that ascend to higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muria mountain pass – View of Llombera loop from Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, starting from a high point and offering panoramic views, making it ideal for those seeking elevated perspectives.

Q: What kind of geological features can be observed in the Vegacervera Gorge?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is a geological marvel. Cyclists passing through can observe impressive vertical limestone cliffs, some reaching up to 500 meters high. The Torío River has carved unique formations like 'marmitas de gigante' (giant's kettles) and 'torcas' or 'dolinas' (sinkholes), showcasing active geological processes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the biosphere reserves in the area?
A: Yes, several routes delve into the region's protected biosphere reserves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037090159/argueellos-biosphere-curueno-and-torio-basins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argüellos Biosphere: Curueño and Torío Basins Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037344610/alto-bernesga-biosphere-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for experiencing the natural beauty and biodiversity of these significant areas.
tips: This section crosses an incredible stone canyon on a national highway. Virtually no cars and some scary views.

Imposing section of the LE-315 provincial road next to the Torío River, which at this point draws some sinuous gorges between some impressive rocky walls. An icy stretch of road if you do it in winter.

Medieval bridge over the Torío river that was rehabilitated in 2004 by the Vegacervera City Council and the León Provincial Council. In addition to consolidating the remains, a wooden footbridge was built over the remaining stone arch to reconnect the two banks.

faqs.question: What level of physical fitness is required for this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and requires a very good physical fitness level. While it includes mostly paved tracks, you will encounter significant elevation changes, including demanding climbs and descents through mountainous valleys.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 48 kilometers long with about 710 meters of elevation gain. On average, it takes around 3 hours and 15 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What are the main natural attractions or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning views through the basins of the Curueño and Torío rivers. You'll pass through admirable gorges like the Hoces de Vegacervera, an imposing stretch with dramatic rocky walls. Another highlight is the Stone canyon on asphalt, which provides breathtaking vistas. You'll also see the historic Vegacervera Bridge.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route is circular within the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Argüellos. It's advisable to look for parking in one of the villages along the route, such as Vegacervera, which is a prominent point of interest.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Argüellos?
faqs.answer: As a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, the area is protected. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or cycling permits required for general access to the natural park itself. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and stay on marked trails.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Biosphere Reserve is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs on this particular cycling route are not provided. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash in natural parks to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences mountainous weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with beautiful landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter may bring snow and colder conditions, making some sections challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on mostly paved tracks (pistas pavimentadas en su mayoría). However, expect significant elevation changes as you navigate through mountainous valleys, gorges, and past vertical rock faces. The landscape is varied, featuring pine and birch forests, beech groves, and distinctive karstic valleys.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino Olvidado (Puente Almuhey-La Robla), Vía Verde Mina Bardaya, and Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, among others. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or drink near the route?
faqs.answer: The villages within the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Argüellos, such as Vegacervera, often have local establishments like cafes and pubs where you can find refreshments and food. These are good spots to refuel during or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The Reserva de la Biosfera de los Argüellos is rich in biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, and if you're lucky, some of the larger mammals native to the Cantabrian mountain ecosystem. Always observe wildlife from a distance and do not disturb their natural habitat.
